entries.title: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked
entries.snippet: Iran is blaming a US blockade for the closure, saying it breaches the ceasefire reached between the two countries.

entries.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
entries.snippet: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

entries.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
entries.snippet: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

entries.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum denies 'diplomatic crisis' with Spain after conquest row
entries.snippet: The Mexican president says there were never tension over Spainish colonisation, which had become a thorny issue.

entries.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
entries.snippet: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

entries.title: French film star Nathalie Baye dies aged 77, media report
entries.snippet: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

entries.title: Building of Trump's White House ballroom can resume in full, appeals court says
entries.snippet: Construction at the White House is expected to continue until the next hearing, due in June.

entries.title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
entries.snippet: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

entries.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
entries.snippet: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

entries.title: HiPP recalls jarred baby food in Austria over contamination fears
entries.snippet: Police are investigating whether jars sold in Spar shops were criminally interfered with.

entries.title: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
entries.snippet: The country's biggest musician Teddy Afro laments a lack of unity in the country on his new track.

entries.title: Three sentenced for 'man in bear suit' insurance scam
entries.snippet: Video went viral after investigators determined the purported ursine was actually a man in a costume.

entries.title: One dead after car hits pedestrians in Melbourne, police say
entries.snippet: A man is arrested following a collision in which police say a car mounted a kerb and struck pedestrians.

entries.title: White House and Anthropic hold 'productive' meeting amid fears over Mythos model
entries.snippet: The discussion is a sign the AI firm's technology may be too critical for even the US government to do without.

entries.title: Orbán's era was over in a flash and Hungary's next PM is a man in a hurry
entries.snippet: Péter Magyar and his Tisza party are wasting no time preparing for the transfer of power after their dramatic landslide victory.

entries.title: What does it take to survive in the Arctic? These rangers have an idea
entries.snippet: The BBC spent time with the Canadian military in the country's northernmost region - this is what they taught us about dealing with extreme conditions.

entries.title: He made jazz under air raids - and built an Indian city's music scene
entries.snippet: War correspondent, jazz bandleader and impresario, KC Sen shaped Kolkata’s music scene.

entries.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
entries.snippet: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

entries.title: What next for South African opposition firebrand Malema after his five-year prison sentence?
entries.snippet: The sentence raises huge questions marks over the political future of one of South Africa's most controversial politicians.

entries.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
entries.snippet: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

entries.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
entries.snippet: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

entries.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
entries.snippet: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

entries.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
entries.snippet: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

entries.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
entries.snippet: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

entries.title: Trump signs order fast tracking review of psychedelics for mental health disorders
entries.snippet: President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"

entries.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
entries.snippet: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

entries.title: Opinion: Remembering Kevin Klose, former NPR president and broadcasting icon
entries.snippet: We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.

entries.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
entries.snippet: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

entries.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
entries.snippet: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

entries.title: She invited her friends to come together to make her casket
entries.snippet: Friends gathered at a weaver's studio in Massachusetts to help MaddyChristine Hope Brokopp make her casket.

entries.title: How consumers can navigate the cost of available flights impacted by the Iran war
entries.snippet: Experts say booking earlier, and staying flexible on dates, destinations and departure airports can help reduce costs.

entries.title: Obama and Mamdani meet for the first time
entries.snippet: The meeting comes as the New York mayor, a democratic socialist, is also trying to build a working relationship with Republican President Donald Trump. The meeting comes just over a week after Mamdani marked his 100th day in office.

entries.title: Witnesses detail deadly Israeli strikes on medics in Lebanon
entries.snippet: The three strikes, which killed four paramedics and wounded six others, is the latest example of Israel's willingness to target Lebanon's health sector. The attacks on the ambulances have drawn condemnation, including from the United Nations' human rights office.

entries.title: Arizona midterms begin with spotlight on largest county's controversial election system
entries.snippet: Much of the political spotlight this election year in Arizona has been focused on the office that runs elections in Maricopa County, the state's largest. Republican Justin Heap is overseeing elections in the Phoenix-area and he's an election skeptic.

entries.title: How to spot the Lyrid meteor shower that is peaking this week
entries.snippet: The show will be visible across the globe, but views will be best in the Northern Hemisphere. And there's no risk of the crescent moon photobombing the Lyrid shower. It'll set before the fun starts.

entries.title: Federal judge blocks Nexstar-Tegna merger until antitrust lawsuit is resolved
entries.snippet: Nexstar's attorneys say the deal has already been vetted and approved by the Federal Communications Commission and the Department of Justice.

entries.title: Iranian official says excessive demands made by U.S. are barrier to in-person talks
entries.snippet: Speaking at a diplomacy forum in Turkey on Saturday, Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh said Iran will not hand over its enriched uranium to the U.S.

entries.title: Ukrainian police fatally shoot gunman who killed at least 6 hostages
entries.snippet: The head of Ukraine's Interior Ministry, Ihor Klymenko, said in a statement on social media that special tactical police units stormed the store after attempts to contact the gunman with a negotiator failed.

entries.title: Trump signs order to hasten review of psychedelics
entries.snippet: Ibogaine and other psychedelics remain banned under the federal government's most restrictive category for illegal, high-risk drugs. But the administration is taking steps to ease restrictions and spur research on using the drugs for medical purposes, including conditions like se

entries.title: Strong winds leave heavy damage in the Midwest
entries.snippet: A series of reported tornadoes has torn through the midwestern United States, ripping roofs off homes and leaving roads impassable. Officials in Illinois, Wisconsin, and Minnesota have expressed relief that the storms did not result in loss of life.

entries.title: Pope Leo arrives in Angola, the third stop on his visit to Africa
entries.snippet: Leo's plane touched down at Luanda's international airport after the flight from Yaounde, Cameroon, where he celebrated a morning Mass before an estimated 200,000 people.

entries.title: Widespread Russian attacks hit Ukraine as Ukraine targets Russian industrial areas
entries.snippet: Local officials report that a civilian was killed and dozens were wounded in overnight Russian attacks across Ukraine.

entries.title: French soldier killed in attack on U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Macron identifies the soldier as Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio from the 17th Parachute Engineer Regiment. He states that Hezbollah is likely responsible for the attack.

entries.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz over U.S. blockade and fires on ships
entries.snippet: The developments come after the United States said it would continue its blockade. Meanwhile, Pakistani mediators are working to organize new negotiations between the countries.

entries.title: After weeks of fighting, ceasefire sparks cautious celebration in Lebanon
entries.snippet: Iran declared that it would reopen the Strait of Hormuz to commercial sea traffic, which President Trump eagerly supported in a social media post. But Trump also declared that the U.S. blockade of Iran's ports could continue. Meanwhile, the day-old truce between Israel and Hezbol

entries.title: Timeline for Strait of Hormuz shipping recovery remains uncertain
entries.snippet: To discuss the status of the Strait of Hormuz and if it's really open to all ships, Amna Nawaz spoke with Ian Ralby. He is president of Auxilium Worldwide, a non-profit organization that focuses on ocean governance and maritime law and security.

entries.title: News Wrap: Senate approves short-term renewal of FISA surveillance program
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Friday, the Senate approved a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, millions across the Midwest are facing the risk of severe storms and French officials say an 85-year-old widow of an American military veteran has

entries.title: Federal judge dismisses DOJ lawsuit seeking personal details about Rhode Island voters
entries.snippet: U.S. District Court Judge Mary McElroy sided with Rhode Island's top election officials and civil rights advocates, writing that federal law does not permit the U.S. Department of Justice "to conduct the kind of fishing expedition it seeks here."

entries.title: How Trump's recent actions are affecting his support among young voters
entries.snippet: President Trump is on the road this week, speaking at a Turning Point USA rally Friday night in Phoenix. White House correspondent Liz Landers on how the administration's recent actions are affecting his support among young voters.

entries.title: Judge sides with Arizona election official in ruling that could affect midterms voting
entries.snippet: The top election official in Arizona's most populous county will get more authority in running elections after a judge sided with his office in a prolonged legal fight with the local board that shares responsibility for overseeing the vote.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Ukraine: Gunman shot dead after killing spree in Kyiv
entries.snippet: The mass shooting, in a busy central district of the Ukrainian capital, left 14 others wounded, officials said.

entries.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
entries.snippet: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

entries.title: Pope Leo plays down rift with Trump, doubles down on peace
entries.snippet: En route to Angola for a three-day visit, Pope Leo XIV said he has no desire to debate with US President Donald Trump. The two leaders have traded barbs since the pope spoke out against the Iran war.

entries.title: US strikes on drug boats spark legal controversy
entries.snippet: The US has carried out further attacks on alleged drug boats. Critics say the strikes are not only ineffective, they are also largely about appealing to Trump's own domestic base.

entries.title: Iran war: Trump says Tehran cannot 'blackmail us'
entries.snippet: President Donald Trump has warned Iran not to "blackmail" the US by shutting the Strait of Hormuz once again. Meanwhile, Tehran has slammed Washington's "maximalist" positions. Follow DW for more.

entries.title: UNIFIL: Why are UN peacekeepers in Lebanon?
entries.snippet: A French UN peacekeeper has been killed in Lebanon, highlighting rising risks for UNIFIL. The UN mission has been in the country for nearly 50 years — DW looks at its mandate, role and growing criticism.

entries.title: India news: Modi slams Congress over women's quota vote
entries.snippet: The Indian prime minister blamed the main opposition party for failing to pass a law reserving a third of parliamentary seats for women. The plan was tied to a redrawing of voting boundaries.

entries.title: British police probe another arson attack in London
entries.snippet: The UK has seen a spate of similar incidents in recent weeks, heightening concerns over growing antisemitism in the country.

entries.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
entries.snippet: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

entries.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
entries.snippet: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

entries.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
entries.snippet: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

entries.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

entries.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
entries.snippet: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

entries.title: Displaced by Iran war: out of Lebanon, into Syrian crisis
entries.snippet: More than 227,000 people have fled conflict in Lebanon for Syria. But there they have found a lack of housing, economic hardship and no real state services. Despite a new ceasefire, many say they will stay in Syria.

entries.title: Germany news: Iran war not causing migration wave — Dobrindt
entries.snippet: German Interior Minister Dobrindt says that no increased migration to Europe has so far occurred amid the Iran war. An explosion in an underpass in a western town has killed one person.

entries.title: US extends waiver allowing purchase of Russian oil
entries.snippet: The US has renewed a waiver permitting countries to buy Russian oil for another month. The decision came two days after the Trump administration had said it had no plans to extend the waiver.

entries.title: World Cup 2026: Fans to pay $150 for train ticket to stadium
entries.snippet: Transportation costs for World Cup fans traveling to US stadiums could be more than 10 times higher than regular fares. Local officials and FIFA bosses are trading blame for the increased prices while fans feel fleeced.

entries.title: Can the 25th Amendment remove Donald Trump from office?
entries.snippet: After Donald Trump threatened to wipe out "an entire civilization," clashed with the pope and posted an AI image of himself as Jesus, US Democrats and some Republicans are questioning his fitness to serve as president.

entries.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
entries.snippet: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.
